intro: Hexasepalum scandens, also known as the climbing hexasepalum or golden dewdrop, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hexasepalum scandens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a decoction or tincture, with the most common herbal preparations being infusions and extracts used to treat various health conditions.
